text: Abstract: AbstractIn the paper, we focus on the implementation of bilingual Czech–Polish nomenclature on the railways in the Zaolzie region. We analyze the socio-cultural position of the Polish national minority in Czech part of Zaolzie region and we describe the role of some important civic associations, such as Congress of Poles in the Czech Republic, which promote the Czech language as well as the Polish and German minority languages in the Czech Republic. In the practical part, we focus on the legislation and theories in relation to bilingualism in the Czech Republic and we analyze the implementation of bilingual nomenclature on some railway stations. We also focused on the use of Polish language in public transport—displaying it on timetables and on electronic announcements. In the conclusion, we summarize the obstacles which still prevent effective implementation of bilingual nomenclature in transport infrastructure in Zaolzie region.
